methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Sublime Systems is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Leah Ellis and Yet-Ming Chiang that has raised over $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-stage, same-sector private companies (Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita) plus one public comparable, with the private universe ranging from $120M to $500M in valuation.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $200M total raised as a proxy anchor and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $380M. Peer-median analysis across the five-company cohort produces a median valuation of $310M, with a range spanning $120M (Cemvita) to $500M (Pano AI). These methods converge on a low estimate of $264M, a median of $345M, and a high of $437M.

The median triangulated valuation of $345M is supported by proximity to peer medians and stage-based progression norms. Key uncertainties include performance dispersion within the peer set—a 4.2x spread from floor to ceiling—and the limited visibility into which peers most closely match Sublime's technical approach and market timing. The valuation range of $264M to $437M reflects this inherent uncertainty in early-stage frontier technology positioning.
